methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Furno Materials is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $11M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer universe of 9 private and 1 public comparable company operating in the same sector, including later-stage peers such as Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, using total capital raised as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $28M. The peer-median method references three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $150M and a range of $110M to $200M, implying $150M. This disparity reflects the wide range of outcomes and capital efficiency typical at early stages in frontier energy sectors.

Triangulation of the two methods yields a valuation range of $23M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $89M. The material spread between the stage-progression and peer-median results reflects competing signals: the stage-progression method anchors on capital deployed, while the peer-median captures valuations of more mature seed/Series A-stage peers. Key uncertainties include Furno's relative technology differentiation versus peers, burn trajectory, and the timing and terms of the next financing round. The $89M median represents a reasonable central estimate pending further due diligence on product-market fit and competitive positioning.
